hypothesis: If an LLM-based agent framework equipped with domain-specific external tools (periodic table, knowledge base, and memory modules) iteratively proposes and refines inorganic material compositions using property feedback, then it will achieve higher success rates in targeting specific formation energies with greater compositional validity, uniqueness, and novelty compared to tool-free LLM variants and non-agentic generative diffusion models, because the structured retrieval of chemical knowledge and iterative self-correction enable more chemically informed and goal-directed exploration of compositional space.
